refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral, specifying what must be achieved without prescribing how. Therefore, it is atomic and does not require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a communication quality monitoring and feedback system to detect packet loss and trigger corrective actions
Incorporate error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication monitoring and control modules could cause coupling if not properly modularized
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical adaptive protocol concept that can be modularized separately from other system functions, supporting independence and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and can be monitored effectively
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that packet loss can be accurately detected and corrected in real-time
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between communication monitoring and control modules to ensure modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for handling communication protocols, monitoring network conditions, and adjusting transmission parameters to maintain communication quality.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends monitored network performance data and status updates to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with the physical and logical network layers to send and receive data packets, enabling adaptive communication adjustments.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to the network gateway solution; minimal diffusion as most related functions are within this module.
Low cohesion since vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle functions; high diffusion as communication protocol would need to interact extensively with other modules.
Moderate cohesion since teleoperation depends on communication quality, but adaptive protocol is more network-centric; moderate diffusion due to cross-module dependencies.
Low cohesion as safety services focus on safety monitoring and alerts; high diffusion if adaptive communication protocol placed here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the adaptive communication protocol DP because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate monitoring and adjustment logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules requiring clear interface definitions.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution module if it also handles unrelated network functions, possibly reducing modular clarity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks materialize, consider refining the DP to separate monitoring and adjustment logic into sub-DPs that can be modularized within NGHE Solution or as a dedicated communication management submodule to improve cohesion and reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, satisfying the acceptance criteria of packet loss ≤ 1% under agreed network conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules requiring clear interface definitions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion (containment score 9) for the adaptive communication protocol DP and minimal diffusion risk (diffusion score 2). The DP is well contained within this module, which manages network gateway and communication management components. However, there is a risk of interface complexity with other modules, and potential overloading if unrelated network functions are included.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module exposes interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) that allow monitoring of network performance and adjustment of transmission parameters, enabling testing against the packet loss acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the adaptive communication protocol DP is fully contained within the NGHE Solution IM boundary, which owns the relevant components and interfaces for teleoperation communication quality.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules and define clear, minimal interfaces to reduce coupling.
If containment risks materialize, refine the DP by decomposing monitoring and adjustment logic into sub-DPs or submodules within NGHE Solution to improve cohesion and reduce diffusion.
Avoid overloading the NGHE Solution module with unrelated network functions to maintain modular clarity and ownership boundaries.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear verification feasibility, allocation clarity, and acceptable containment and diffusion scores. The adaptive communication protocol DP fully addresses the FR with a well-contained IM module (NGHE Solution) that supports monitoring and adjustment to meet packet loss criteria. Although there is a noted risk of interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules, this is manageable through interface definition and does not warrant further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and cohesive module boundary is essential, and current design supports this. Therefore, no further sub-FR derivation is needed now; focus should be on monitoring interface complexity and avoiding module overloading as recommended.

revise.why: To monitor and manage interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules by defining clear, minimal interfaces and avoiding overloading the NGHE Solution module with unrelated network functions.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully satisfies the FR under agreed network conditions.
The NGHE Solution module ownership and interfaces are stable and well-defined.
Interface complexity risks can be mitigated through clear interface definitions without further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules requiring careful interface management.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution module if unrelated network functions are added.
evidence_needed: Verification test results confirming packet loss ≤ 1% under operational conditions.
Interface definition documents between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle modules.
Monitoring reports on module cohesion and diffusion during integration.
